title: Cantonment Church Tower
tagline: Cantonment Church Tower — spiritual town of Haryana

overview.short: The Cantonment Church Tower is a church tower that was formerly part of St.
overview.description: The Cantonment Church Tower is a church tower that was formerly part of St. James' Church, a former Anglican church, located in Karnal city of Haryana, India. The 35-metre (115 ft) tower was built as an addition to the former church that was founded in 1806, and is now an historical religious shrine in Karnal. The tower stands near National Highway 1 of India and is listed by the Indian National Trust for Art and Cultural Heritage as an historical monument.

topPlaces.name: Karnal division
topPlaces.description: Karnal Division is one of the six divisions of Haryana, a state of India. It comprises the districts of Karnal, Panipat and Kaithal.

topPlaces.name: Karnal
topPlaces.description: Karnal (Punjabi pronunciation: [ˌkʌ̯ˈɾ.ˈnɐː˩˥l]) is a city located in the Indian state of Haryana and serves as the administrative headquarters of the Karnal district. The city lies on National Highway 44, which connects it to the national capital, New Delhi, and the state capital, Chandigarh, placing Karnal almost midway between the two.

topPlaces.name: Immaculate Heart Church, Karnal
topPlaces.description: The Church of Immaculate Heart of Mary usually known as Immaculate Heart Church, Karnal is located in Karnal city of Haryana, India. It stands on Kunjpura Road near National Highway 1 of India besides the well known St.

topPlaces.name: Karnal Aerodrome
topPlaces.description: Karnal Aerodrome is operated as a pilot training institute owned and operated by the Haryana Institute of Civil Aviation (HICA) under the guidance of the Civil Aviation Department, Government of Haryana. The flying school covers an area of 104 acres and is situated about 3 km east of Karnal in the state of Haryana, India.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Karnal
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Karnal (Persian: نبرد کرنال) (24 February 1739) was a decisive victory for Nader Shah, the founder of the Afsharid dynasty of Iran, during his invasion of India. Nader's forces defeated the army of Mughal emperor Muhammad Shah within three hours, paving the way for the Iranian sack of Delhi.

topPlaces.name: Hemda
topPlaces.description: Hemda is a village in Karnal district of state Haryana, India. It is located on SH-8 between Karnal to Kaithal.

topPlaces.name: Kunjpura
topPlaces.description: Kunjpura is a town in the Karnal district of the Indian state of Haryana, about 10 km northeast of Karnal city and about 130 km north of the national capital, Delhi. It is on the right bank (west bank) of the Yamuna River, off the Grand Trunk Road that runs from Amritsar to Delhi and further on to Calcutta.

topPlaces.name: Taraori
topPlaces.description: Taraori, or Tarori or Tarawari, as it is sometimes called in the local dialect, is a town (Municipal committee) in Nilokheri Tehsil of Karnal district in the Indian state of Haryana. It is situated off NH-44, 14 km north of Karnal.

items.note: Karnal Division is one of the six divisions of Haryana, a state of India. It comprises the districts of Karnal, Panipat and Kaithal.

Karnal (Punjabi pronunciation: [ˌkʌ̯ˈɾ.ˈnɐː˩˥l]) is a city located in the Indian state of Haryana and serves as the administrative headquarters of the Karnal district. The city lies on National Highway 44, which connects it to the national capital, New Delhi, and the state capital, Chandigarh, placing Karnal almost midway between the two.

The Church of Immaculate Heart of Mary usually known as Immaculate Heart Church, Karnal is located in Karnal city of Haryana, India. It stands on Kunjpura Road near National Highway 1 of India besides the well known St.

Karnal Aerodrome is operated as a pilot training institute owned and operated by the Haryana Institute of Civil Aviation (HICA) under the guidance of the Civil Aviation Department, Government of Haryana. The flying school covers an area of 104 acres and is situated about 3 km east of Karnal in the state of Haryana, India.

The Battle of Karnal (Persian: نبرد کرنال) (24 February 1739) was a decisive victory for Nader Shah, the founder of the Afsharid dynasty of Iran, during his invasion of India. Nader's forces defeated the army of Mughal emperor Muhammad Shah within three hours, paving the way for the Iranian sack of Delhi.
Hemda is a village in Karnal district of state Haryana, India. It is located on SH-8 between Karnal to Kaithal.

Kunjpura is a town in the Karnal district of the Indian state of Haryana, about 10 km northeast of Karnal city and about 130 km north of the national capital, Delhi. It is on the right bank (west bank) of the Yamuna River, off the Grand Trunk Road that runs from Amritsar to Delhi and further on to Calcutta.

Taraori, or Tarori or Tarawari, as it is sometimes called in the local dialect, is a town (Municipal committee) in Nilokheri Tehsil of Karnal district in the Indian state of Haryana. It is situated off NH-44, 14 km north of Karnal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cantonment Church Tower?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cantonment Church Tower is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cantonment Church Tower?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (109 km) and the nearest railway station is Ambala Cantt Junction (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cantonment Church Tower?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cantonment Church Tower at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cantonment Church Tower?
faq.a: Stays in Cantonment Church Tower start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cantonment Church Tower?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karnal division, Karnal, Immaculate Heart Church, Karnal, Karnal Aerodrome, Battle of Karnal.
